untranslatable_reason: This signal is a meta-rule about system design principles (requiring integrated exits, position sizing, and backtested expectancy) rather than a tradeable pattern with specific price action or indicator conditions. It cannot be expressed as concrete entry/exit conditions using technical indicators. The rule requires: (1) pattern identification (unspecified which pattern), (2) exit rule validation across 100+ trades, (3) position sizing integration with 1% max risk, and (4) positive expectancy confirmation. These are system architecture requirements, not indicator-based signals. Implementation requires custom backtesting validation logic outside the scope of indicator-based rules.
translation_notes: Signal THARP_DRY_18_0 is a methodological principle emphasizing system completeness rather than a specific entry/exit pattern. To implement: (1) Define the actual pattern to be recognized, (2) Pair with a specific exit rule, (3) Apply position sizing with max 1% risk per trade, (4) Validate combined system on minimum 100 historical trades showing positive mathematical expectancy. The rule itself serves as a filter or meta-condition applied after pattern identification.
